refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refinement clarifies that availability is a measurable, time-based metric with explicit exclusions (planned maintenance) and inclusions (safety-driven stops). High safety criticality demands that availability measurement does not mask safety failures; safety-driven unavailability is acceptable and must be distinguished from reliability failures.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of system state transitions (operational, maintenance, fault, safety-hold) with timestamped records. Calculate availability as (total_scheduled_time - unplanned_downtime - safety_holds) / total_scheduled_time. Compare against 0.97 threshold at end of evaluation period. Require independent audit trail.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The system shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults) with root cause and duration.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The system shall distinguish and separately track safety-driven unavailability from reliability-driven unavailability.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The system shall provide real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance personnel.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The system shall implement redundancy and fault tolerance mechanisms to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The system shall support predictive maintenance scheduling to reduce unplanned failures.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and reporting system that continuously tracks system state, logs downtime events, calculates availability metrics in real-time, and alerts stakeholders when availability falls below target.

DP-B: Distribute availability monitoring across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, each reporting local uptime; aggregate at NGHE Solution level with post-hoc availability calculation.

DP-C: Implement redundant subsystems (dual motors, dual control channels, dual safety circuits) with automatic failover to eliminate single points of failure; couple with event logging for root cause analysis.
DP-D: Combine centralized monitoring (DP-A) with redundant subsystems (DP-C) and predictive maintenance scheduling to proactively reduce downtime.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a three-pillar availability assurance strategy: (1) Centralized real-time availability monitoring system that tracks state transitions, logs all downtime events with timestamps and root causes, calculates rolling availability metrics, and triggers alerts when thresholds are breached; (2) Redundant critical subsystems (dual propulsion, dual control channels, dual safety circuits) with automatic failover logic to minimize unplanned downtime; (3) Predictive maintenance scheduling engine that analyzes telemetry trends to forecast component failures and schedule maintenance before failures occur, reducing unplanned downtime.

potential_sources: Monitoring system must receive telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services—requires well-defined Telemetry interface.
Redundancy logic must coordinate with Safety Services to ensure failover does not violate safety constraints.
Predictive maintenance scheduling must integrate with Maintenance/Quality stakeholder workflows and operational planning.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is decoupled from specific hardware implementations; it prescribes logical architecture (monitoring, redundancy, prediction) that can be realized across multiple modules. Coupling is managed through explicit interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert) and stakeholder coordination. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Telemetry data from all subsystems is reliable and timestamped accurately. Risk: Clock skew or data loss could corrupt availability calculations. Mitigation: Implement synchronized time sources and data validation.

Assumption: Redundant subsystems can be designed and tested to fail independently. Risk: Common-mode failures (e.g., software bug affecting both channels) could defeat redundancy. Mitigation: Diverse design, independent testing, formal verification of failover logic.

Assumption: Predictive maintenance models can be trained on historical data and generalize to new equipment. Risk: Model drift or insufficient training data could lead to false positives/negatives. Mitigation: Continuous model validation, human-in-the-loop review, conservative thresholds.

Assumption: Safety-driven unavailability can be cleanly separated from reliability-driven unavailability in metrics. Risk: Ambiguous edge cases (e.g., safety shutdown due to sensor fault) could be miscategorized. Mitigation: Define clear categorization rules and audit trail.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but should be refined to explicitly state: (a) Availability metric definition and calculation method (e.g., rolling 30-day window, exclusions for planned maintenance); (b) Redundancy strategy scope (which subsystems are redundant, which are not); (c) Predictive maintenance trigger thresholds and escalation procedures; (d) Integration points with Safety Services to ensure redundancy does not compromise safety.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the availability monitoring and reporting system, coordinates redundancy logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, integrates predictive maintenance scheduling, and provides availability metrics to stakeholders. It does not own the physical redundant subsystems (those are owned by Vehicle and Teleoperation) but orchestrates their operation and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives continuous telemetry streams from Vehicle (propulsion, control, sensor status) and Teleoperation (remote control status, latency, link quality). Used to populate availability monitoring system and feed predictive maintenance models.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-critical alerts from Safety Services (e.g., emergency stop, safety constraint violations). Used to categorize unavailability as safety-driven vs. reliability-driven and to coordinate failover logic with safety constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends availability status and maintenance scheduling recommendations to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders. Includes alerts when availability falls below target and predictive maintenance notifications.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to query state, trigger failover commands, and receive status updates. Also used to synchronize time across distributed subsystems for accurate timestamp correlation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level availability orchestration, monitoring, and reporting. It integrates data from Vehicle and Teleoperation (via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces) and provides visibility to stakeholders (via Tasking). Minimal diffusion: only the redundant subsystem implementations are owned by Vehicle and Teleoperation; the orchestration logic resides here. High containment because availability is a system-level KPI, not a subsystem concern.

Vehicle owns the physical redundant propulsion and control subsystems but does not own the availability monitoring or orchestration logic. If availability logic were distributed to Vehicle, it would be tightly coupled to Vehicle-specific implementations and would not generalize to Teleoperation or other subsystems. High diffusion risk: availability is a system-level concern, not a vehicle-level concern.

Teleoperation owns remote control redundancy but not system-level availability orchestration. Similar to Vehicle, assigning availability logic to Teleoperation would create diffusion and coupling. Teleoperation should report its local availability status via Telemetry interface; NGHE Solution aggregates.

Safety Services owns safety-critical logic and alerts but not availability monitoring or redundancy orchestration. Safety Services should provide SafetyAlert signals to NGHE Solution; NGHE Solution uses these to categorize unavailability and coordinate failover. Assigning availability logic to Safety Services would conflate safety and availability concerns.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because: (1) Availability is a system-level KPI that requires integration of data from multiple subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (2) Monitoring and orchestration logic is subsystem-agnostic and should not be duplicated; (3) Stakeholder reporting (Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality) is directed at the system level, not subsystems; (4) Redundancy failover logic must coordinate across subsystems, requiring a system-level orchestrator. Containment score of 0.95 reflects high alignment with NGHE Solution's scope.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck if it owns too much orchestration logic. Mitigation: Implement NGHE Solution as a lightweight coordinator that delegates subsystem-specific logic to Vehicle and Teleoperation; use event-driven architecture to avoid tight coupling.

Risk: Availability monitoring system may require real-time performance (low latency, high throughput) that conflicts with other NGHE Solution responsibilities. Mitigation: Implement monitoring as a separate service or thread with dedicated resources; use asynchronous communication.

Risk: Predictive maintenance models may require domain expertise (e.g., mechanical engineering, machine learning) that is not available in NGHE Solution team. Mitigation: Partner with Maintenance/Quality stakeholders to develop and validate models; implement as a pluggable service.

Risk: Redundancy failover logic may interact with Safety Services in complex ways (e.g., safety constraint prevents failover). Mitigation: Define clear coordination protocol between NGHE Solution and Safety Services; use formal methods to verify safety properties.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution assignment is sound. Recommend: (1) Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules for monitoring, redundancy orchestration, and predictive maintenance to avoid monolithic design; (2) Define clear interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation for redundancy failover commands; (3) Establish governance for predictive maintenance model updates and validation; (4) Implement monitoring system with redundancy and fault tolerance to ensure it does not become a single point of failure.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (three-pillar availability assurance) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP specifies concrete mechanisms (monitoring, redundancy, predictive maintenance) that, if implemented correctly, will enable the system to meet the availability target. The DP is measurable and verifiable: availability metrics can be calculated from monitoring data, redundancy effectiveness can be tested, and predictive maintenance accuracy can be validated. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If redundancy logic is not carefully encapsulated in NGHE Solution, it may diffuse into Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, creating coupling and duplication. Mitigation: Define clear API for redundancy failover commands; Vehicle and Teleoperation should implement failover handlers, not failover logic.

Risk: If monitoring system is not properly abstracted, it may become tightly coupled to specific telemetry formats from Vehicle and Teleoperation. Mitigation: Define canonical Telemetry interface with versioning; implement adapters if subsystems use different formats.

Risk: If predictive maintenance models are trained on Vehicle-specific data, they may not generalize to Teleoperation or other subsystems. Mitigation: Develop subsystem-agnostic models (e.g., generic failure modes) and subsystem-specific models (e.g., motor-specific degradation); implement as separate services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of availability orchestration. Vehicle and Teleoperation own redundant subsystems but delegate availability monitoring and orchestration to NGHE Solution via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces. This separation of concerns minimizes diffusion and maximizes reusability. No evidence of misalignment between DP and IM.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-004 is feasible via NGHE Solution's monitoring system. Availability metrics can be calculated from timestamped state transition logs. Verification requires: (1) Instrumentation of all subsystems to report state changes to monitoring system; (2) Validation of monitoring system's accuracy (e.g., comparison with manual logs); (3) Long-term data collection over the agreed evaluation period; (4) Independent audit of availability calculations. Feasibility is high because monitoring is a standard engineering practice.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of availability responsibility is clear: NGHE Solution owns the monitoring, orchestration, and reporting; Vehicle and Teleoperation own redundant subsystems and report status via Telemetry; Safety Services provides SafetyAlert signals. Each module has a well-defined role. Stakeholder responsibilities are also clear: Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality use availability metrics to make operational decisions; System Engineer and Project Manager use metrics to assess system health and plan improvements.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to specify availability metric definition (e.g., rolling 30-day window, exclusions for planned maintenance, categorization rules for safety-driven unavailability).
Define redundancy strategy scope: which subsystems are redundant (e.g., propulsion, control), which are not (e.g., sensors), and what level of redundancy (e.g., 1oo2, 2oo3).
Establish predictive maintenance trigger thresholds and escalation procedures (e.g., alert at 80% confidence of failure, schedule maintenance at 90% confidence).
Define coordination protocol between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for redundancy failover under safety constraints.
Specify monitoring system architecture (e.g., centralized vs. distributed, real-time vs. batch processing) and performance requirements (e.g., latency, throughput).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs to enable detailed design and implementation. The refined FR identifies five sub-FRs (detection/logging, categorization, visibility, redundancy, predictive maintenance) that address different aspects of availability assurance. DP-D and NGHE Solution assignment are sound and pass all gates, but further decomposition is necessary to allocate responsibility to specific modules and design concrete mechanisms. Proceeding to the next level of decomposition will enable detailed design of monitoring system, redundancy logic, and predictive maintenance models.
decision.justification_detail: The current level of decomposition is appropriate for system architecture and high-level design. However, to proceed to detailed design and implementation, each sub-FR must be further decomposed into atomic FRs with corresponding DPs and IMs. For example, FR-NGHE-004.1 (detect and log unplanned downtime) requires decomposition into FRs for fault detection, event logging, root cause analysis, etc. This will enable assignment of responsibility to specific modules (e.g., Vehicle owns fault detection for propulsion, Safety Services owns fault detection for safety-critical functions) and design of concrete mechanisms (e.g., sensor monitoring, event database, diagnostic algorithms).
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The system shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults) with root cause and duration.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The system shall distinguish and separately track safety-driven unavailability from reliability-driven unavailability.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The system shall provide real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance personnel.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The system shall implement redundancy and fault tolerance mechanisms to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The system shall support predictive maintenance scheduling to reduce unplanned failures.

assumptions: Telemetry data from all subsystems is reliable, complete, and accurately timestamped.
Redundant subsystems can be designed to fail independently (no common-mode failures).
Predictive maintenance models can be trained on historical data and generalize to new equipment.
Safety-driven unavailability can be cleanly separated from reliability-driven unavailability.
Stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality) will actively use availability metrics to make operational decisions.
Customer-defined availability target is ≥0.97 or will be specified during requirements refinement.
open_risks: Risk: Monitoring system becomes a single point of failure if not designed with redundancy. Mitigation: Implement monitoring with redundant data collection and processing.
Risk: Redundancy failover logic interacts with Safety Services in unexpected ways. Mitigation: Formal verification of safety properties, extensive testing.

Risk: Predictive maintenance models produce false positives/negatives, leading to unnecessary maintenance or missed failures. Mitigation: Conservative thresholds, human-in-the-loop review, continuous model validation.
Risk: Availability metrics are gamed or manipulated (e.g., by excluding downtime from calculations). Mitigation: Independent audit trail, stakeholder oversight, clear categorization rules.
Risk: Evaluation period is too short to achieve statistically significant availability measurement. Mitigation: Agree on evaluation period length with customer; use confidence intervals in reporting.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of availability metric definition and calculation method.
Redundancy strategy document specifying which subsystems are redundant and at what level.
Predictive maintenance model specifications and validation results.
Coordination protocol between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for redundancy failover.
Monitoring system architecture and performance requirements.
Test plan for availability assurance mechanisms (monitoring, redundancy, predictive maintenance).
Historical data or simulation results demonstrating that DP-D can achieve ≥0.97 availability target.
